title: syringodea longituba subsp. longituba
intro: Syringodea longituba subsp. longituba is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae, commonly known as the "Red-hot Poker" or "Synges". This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Syringodea longituba subsp. longituba include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, with the dried roots and leaves being the most commonly used parts.
